text:
Abstract Pyrazole derivatives have been reported to display a wide spectrum of biological activities such as antibacterial, antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and anticancer activities. In this study, the synthesized of two novel derivatives of pyrazole 2-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazole-3-carbonyl)-N-phenylhydrazine-1-carboxamide (Pyz-1) and 4-amino-5-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thiol (Pyz-2) have been identified by 1H-NMR, 13C-NMR, FT-IR and mass spectrometry. The calculated densities and enthalpies based on density functional theory (DFT) at the B3LYP/6-311++G** level have been employed to predict molecular structure, energy levels and thermodynamic parameters in gas and solution phases.

Here, we investigated the bioactivities of new synthesized pyrazoline derivatives (Pyz-1 and Pyz-2). Our data showed that the two compounds possessed not only the excellent antidiabetic and antioxidant activities. IC50 values of Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 for inhibiting α-glucosidase and α-amylase were 75.62–95.85 µM and 119.3–120.2 µM, respectively, which were close to that of Acarbose. Also, both compounds showed strong xanthine oxidase inhibition activity, and that Pyz-2 showed stronger inhibitory activity than the commercial drug Allopurinol with IC50 value of 10.75 µM. Meanwhile, the two compounds exhibited strong radical scavenging activities against DPPH, ABTS, FRAP and H2O2 assays. Besides, molecular docking studies illustrated that Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 interacted with α-glucosidase and α-amylase through binding energies and protein-ligand interactions. Collectively, the results supported that Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 possessed multi-target bioactivities toward diabetes and oxidative stress.
explanation: The text is highly technical and coherent with specific data, which can be produced by either humans or AI; the minor grammar hiccups (e.g., 'the synthesized of') suggest possible AI authorship with imperfect editing.
